key_insight: PyroDash teaches a small language model to decide, token by token, when to hand off reasoning to a frozen large model, achieving controllable accuracy–cost tradeoffs.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical serving strategy for math-heavy LLM workloads that lowers inference spend without requiring a separate router, LLM retraining, or access to LLM logits.
method: SLM emits a control token to request assistance during generation.
A Collaborate Engine passes the query plus partial reasoning trace to a frozen LLM in a single handoff.
Training uses control-token embedding learning, supervised fine-tuning, and cost-aware GRPO alignment.
evidence_signal: On five mathematical reasoning benchmarks, PyroDash reached 64.04% average accuracy at λ=0.05 with 20.4% lower cost, and 54.55% accuracy at λ=0.6 with only 1.90% LLM token ratio and 0.012 LLM calls per example.
watch_item: Performance depends on learning a reliable handoff policy in the SLM, so gains may be sensitive to benchmark shift, token-budget settings, or unstable cost–accuracy tuning.
paper_title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
